name: Courant-Friedrichs-Lewy stability condition for explicit hyperbolic schemes
conventions: u is the maximum wave / advection speed in the system
delta t is the time step; delta x is the spatial grid spacing
C_max is method-dependent: C_max = 1 for first-order upwind on the linear advection equation; smaller for higher-order or multi-dimensional schemes
in 2D / 3D: C = max_d (|u_d| delta t / delta x_d), summed over dimensions for some schemes
applies to explicit time-stepping; implicit schemes can be stable for any C but pay per-step solver cost
expectedLimits: C < C_max:  scheme is stable for the linear test problem (necessary, not always sufficient — von Neumann analysis confirms)
C = C_max:  marginal stability; small perturbations may grow due to round-off
C > C_max:  exponential instability; the discrete domain of dependence excludes the continuous one
delta t, delta x -> 0 with C fixed:  consistent + stable -> convergent (Lax equivalence theorem)
diffusion-only equations (parabolic): different stability bound, e.g. C_diff = D delta t / delta x^2 <= 1/2 for explicit forward Euler
stiff systems: explicit CFL forces impractically small delta t -> use implicit / IMEX schemes
references: Courant, Friedrichs & Lewy (1928) — Über die partiellen Differenzengleichungen der mathematischen Physik
Lax & Wendroff (1960) — Systems of conservation laws
LeVeque — Finite Difference Methods for Ordinary and Partial Differential Equations, ch.10
Strikwerda — Finite Difference Schemes and Partial Differential Equations, ch.1
